skill.name: social-content
skill.description: Expert social media strategist for creating, scheduling, and optimizing content across LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook
skill.summary: AI-powered social media content strategist with platform-specific templates, analytics guidance, and engagement strategies
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 36 potential issues across external_commands, cryptographic patterns, and system reconnaissance. All findings are false positives. The backtick patterns are markdown code block delimiters for content templates, not executable code. Cryptographic and reconnaissance flags result from pattern matching on innocent text (content strategy instructions, analytics checkboxes). This is a legitimate educational skill with no security concerns.

content.user_title: Create Viral Social Media Content with AI
content.value_statement: Struggling to create engaging social content consistently? This skill provides platform-specific templates, proven formulas, and strategic guidance to help you build audience, drive engagement, and achieve your business goals across LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook.
actual_capabilities: Generate platform-specific content templates for LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook
Create viral hook formulas using curiosity, story, value, contrarian, and social proof patterns
Develop content calendar structures with weekly batching strategies
Provide engagement strategies including proactive outreach and relationship building
Offer analytics frameworks to track metrics and optimize content performance
Reverse engineer viral content with systematic 6-step analysis framework
limitations: Does not directly post or schedule content to social media platforms
Cannot access real-time analytics or engagement data from your accounts
Requires manual implementation of suggested strategies and templates
Platform algorithms and best practices change over time, requiring ongoing adaptation

use_cases.title: Founder Building Personal Brand
use_cases.description: A startup founder wants to establish thought leadership on LinkedIn while engaging with tech communities on Twitter/X. Use this skill to create a content mix of industry insights, behind-the-scenes content, and educational posts that build credibility and attract potential customers.

use_cases.title: Marketing Team Scaling Content Production
use_cases.description: A small marketing team needs to produce consistent content across multiple platforms without burnout. Use the batching templates and repurposing frameworks to turn one blog post into LinkedIn carousels, Twitter threads, and Instagram Reels.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Brand Driving Sales
use_cases.description: An online retailer wants to use social content to drive traffic and conversions. Use the conversion-focused templates and analytics guidance to create content that moves followers through the funnel from awareness to purchase.

prompt_templates.title: Create LinkedIn Post from Story
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write a LinkedIn post using the story formula. I want to share [specific experience/lesson]. Here's what happened: [brief details]. Use the story template structure with a hook, setup, challenge, turning point, result, and lesson.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a personal experience or business lesson to share on LinkedIn

prompt_templates.title: Generate Twitter Thread from Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this [blog post/article/idea] into a Twitter/X thread. Use the tutorial thread format with a strong hook in tweet 1, then break down the key points step-by-step across the thread. End with a summary and clear CTA.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to repurpose existing long-form content into Twitter thread

prompt_templates.title: Develop Content Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a social media content strategy for [business/personal brand]. My target audience is [description]. My main goals are [specific goals]. Suggest content pillars, weekly posting schedule, and content mix for [platforms].
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs comprehensive social media strategy and planning

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Underperforming Content
prompt_templates.prompt: My recent posts have been getting low engagement. Here's an example post: [paste post]. Analyze what might not be working and suggest 3 improvements based on platform best practices, hook formulas, and engagement strategies.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to improve content performance based on analytics

output_examples.input: Help me write a LinkedIn post about how I doubled my engagement by stopping daily posting
output_examples.output: I stopped posting daily and my engagement doubled. Here's what happened: I was burning out creating 7 posts/week that nobody saw. When I cut to 3 high-quality posts, engagement per post tripled. The algorithm rewarded quality over quantity. Which resonates more: frequency or quality?

output_examples.input: Create a Twitter thread about common pricing mistakes
output_examples.output: 7 pricing mistakes that cost founders $50K+ 1/ Underpricing to 'get traction' - signals low value, attracts wrong customers 2/ No tiered options - leaves money on table from budget buyers 3/ Hidden fees - kills trust instantly 4/ Annual only - creates too much friction 5/ No anchoring - misses psychological pricing advantage 6/ Raising too slowly - inflation eats margins 7/ No testing - guessing instead of measuring Stop guessing. Test your way to optimal pricing.

best_practices: Provide context about your goals, audience, and brand voice before asking for content generation
Use the platform-specific templates rather than copying the same content across all platforms
Focus on authentic stories and data-backed insights rather than generic motivational content
Batch create content during dedicated sessions rather than trying to create daily on the fly
anti_patterns: Don't use the skill to create spammy, overly promotional, or misleading content
Avoid posting identical content across platforms without adapting to each platform's format and audience
Don't skip the engagement strategy section - social media requires two-way communication, not just broadcasting
Don't expect immediate viral results - building an audience takes consistent application of these strategies over time

faq.question: Does this skill actually post content to social media platforms?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides strategic guidance, templates, and content suggestions. You'll need to manually post the generated content or use a separate scheduling tool. The skill helps you create better content, not automate publishing.

faq.question: Which platform should I focus on first?
faq.answer: Start with one platform where your target audience is most active. LinkedIn works well for B2B and professional networking, Twitter/X for tech and real-time discourse, Instagram for visual brands. Master one before expanding to others.

faq.question: How often should I post based on the recommendations?
faq.answer: Quality matters more than quantity. The skill recommends 3-5 LinkedIn posts per week, 3-10 tweets per day, or 1-2 Instagram feed posts daily. Consistency is key - choose a sustainable schedule you can maintain long-term.

faq.question: Can I use these templates for any industry?
faq.answer: Yes, the templates and frameworks work across industries. The key is adapting them to your specific audience, brand voice, and expertise. Use the strategy section to define your unique content pillars before applying the templates.

faq.question: What if my content isn't getting engagement?
faq.answer: Use the analytics and optimization section to review performance. Common issues include weak hooks, posting at wrong times, or content not matching audience interests. Test different hooks, formats, and topics based on your actual data.

faq.question: Do I need to be on all social media platforms?
faq.answer: No, focus on platforms where your target audience spends time. B2B companies often succeed on LinkedIn alone. Visual brands may prioritize Instagram. Choose based on your audience demographics and where you can create content consistently.
